title: Make checkpointing work when prefix contains non-ascii characters

labels.description: Where Oceananigans gets its powers from
labels.name: GPU 👾

title: Bump the compat on GPUArrays to 11.2.2

body:
This PR enables interpolating between `LatitudeLongitudeGrid`s with different longitude references provided the covered longitude is the same

example:
```julia
julia> grid1 = LatitudeLongitudeGrid(size=(10, 1, 1), longitude=(   0, 360), latitude=(-90, 90), z=(0, 1));

julia> grid2 = LatitudeLongitudeGrid(size=(10, 1, 1), longitude=(-180, 180), latitude=(-90, 90), z=(0, 1));

julia> f1 = CenterField(grid1);

julia> f2 = CenterField(grid2);

julia> set!(f1, (λ, y, z) -> λ);

julia> fill_halo_regions!(f1);

julia> interpolate!(f2, f1);

julia> interior(f2)
10×1×1 view(::Array{Float64, 3}, 4:13, 2:2, 2:2) with eltype Float64:
[:, :, 1] =
 198.0
 234.0
 270.0
 306.0
 342.0
  18.0
  54.0
  90.0
 126.0
 162.0

julia> fill_halo_regions!(f2);

julia> interpolate!(f1, f2);

julia> interior(f1)
10×1×1 view(::Array{Float64, 3}, 4:13, 2:2, 2:2) with eltype Float64:
[:, :, 1] =
  18.0
  54.0
  90.0
 126.0
 162.0
 198.0
 234.0
 270.0
 306.0
 342.0
```

Needed, for example, to perform Atlantic and Mediterranean sea simulations in ClimaOcean.
title: Correct interpolation on longitude lines

body:
This PR implements a new `Defaults` struct with value `Oceananigans.defaults`.

The design is that `defaults` are used in constructors, so changing the content of `Oceananigans.defaults` at the top of a setup script or in a REPL session can change constructed objects afterwards that use the default. For example:

```julia
using Oceananigans
Oceananigans.defaults.float_type = Float32
```

then

```julia
julia> grid = RectilinearGrid(size=(3, 3, 3), extent=(1, 1, 1))
3×3×3 RectilinearGrid{Float32, Periodic, Periodic, Bounded} on CPU with 3×3×3 halo
├── Periodic x ∈ [0.0, 1.0)  regularly spaced with Δx=0.333333
├── Periodic y ∈ [0.0, 1.0)  regularly spaced with Δy=0.333333
└── Bounded  z ∈ [-1.0, 0.0] regularly spaced with Δz=0.333333
```

But of course the default can still be overridden as usual

```julia
julia> grid = RectilinearGrid(Float64, size=(3, 3, 3), extent=(1, 1, 1))
3×3×3 RectilinearGrid{Float64, Periodic, Periodic, Bounded} on CPU with 3×3×3 halo
├── Periodic x ∈ [0.0, 1.0)  regularly spaced with Δx=0.333333
├── Periodic y ∈ [0.0, 1.0)  regularly spaced with Δy=0.333333
└── Bounded  z ∈ [-1.0, 0.0] regularly spaced with Δz=0.333333
```

Closes #3800 

Note, I did not add a constructor of the form `set_default_number_type!`. Still, users cannot write anything since the design is based on a mutable `Defaults` struct, so

```julia
julia> Oceananigans.defaults = "Float64"
ERROR: invalid redefinition of constant Oceananigans.defaults
Stacktrace:
 [1] setproperty!
   @ ./Base.jl:51 [inlined]
 [2] setproperty!(x::Module, f::Symbol, v::String)
   @ Base ./Base.jl:49
 [3] top-level scope
   @ REPL[12]:1
```

fails. We could possibly catch these settings and throw errors like "it has to be a DataType". I don't want to retstrict the datatype that migth be used because there are experimental datatypes, for example BFloat16, and who knows what in the future.

TODO:

- [ ] tests
title: Implement a default float type

body:
Apparently this is required for package deps to declare all their deps.

Hopefully resolves the extension loading issue, but irrespective thereof this should have been done anyways
title: Reactant: try to fix extension loading
